ground_truth_fix: You are using bpf_trace_printk incorrectly, you typically stack allocate the fmt, then take the sizeof the char array and pass it as the second argument. I recommend you switch all usages to bpf_printk from bpf/bpf_helpers.h, since it does work exactly like you are invoking it right now. This macro does all of the heavy lifting for you.
